Course Title: AI in Education: When Learning Becomes Dependency
Category: Education & Public Sector: Education, Government Services, Non-Profit organizations
Who is this for: Teachers, Educators, Academic Administrators, School Principals, Training Coordinators, Learning and Development Professionals, Curriculum Directors, Educational Technology Specialists
Team or Dept this is for: Education, Training and Development, Academic Affairs, Curriculum Development, Student Services, Educational Technology
Description: Risks of students relying on AI instead of developing core skills.
Prerequisites: Learners should have a basic familiarity with using digital tools or online platforms, and an interest in how artificial intelligence is beginning to shape teaching and learning environments.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key risks associated with student dependency on AI tools and explain how over-reliance on these technologies can hinder the development of essential academic and critical thinking skills. You will also apply practical strategies to recognise early signs of AI dependency in learning environments and support students in building genuine, lasting competencies alongside responsible AI use.
